# QSBS Facts

> Data-driven analysis of Section 1202 Qualified Small Business Stock (QSBS) tax policy. Argues against state-level QSBS decoupling using U.S. Treasury data, academic studies, and migration statistics.

URL: https://qsbsfacts.org

## What This Site Covers

QSBS Facts presents evidence-based analysis of IRC Section 1202 — the federal tax exclusion for qualified small business stock. The site counters the narrative that QSBS is a "tax loophole for millionaires" by examining actual Treasury data on who claims the exclusion, how often, and how much.

## Key Data Points (U.S. Treasury OTA Working Paper 127, January 2025)

- **75% of QSBS claimants** used the exclusion exactly once across 11 years (2012–2022). Only 6% claimed it five or more years.
- **74% of claimants** earn under $1 million (by average income, excluding QSBS gains). The largest group (35%) earns $100K–$400K.
- **Median annual exclusion: $2,810.** 90th percentile is $590,940. This is not a large-dollar benefit for most claimants.
- **QSBS was 2.55% of all capital gains** in 2021 — a rounding error in total capital gains activity.
- **$152 billion** in total exclusions claimed from 2012–2022, peaking at $51.1B in 2021.
- **The "94% goes to millionaires" claim** (from ITEP) combines two groups: 74% of dollars going to people already over $1M, and 20% going to people whose one-time exit temporarily pushed them over $1M. The second group is exactly who QSBS was designed for.

## Academic Evidence

- Edwards & Todenhaupt (2020): Startup investment increased ~12% after the 100% exclusion was enacted in 2010.
- Chen & Farre-Mensa (2023/2025): QSBS-eligible industries experienced more firm births, more startup employment, and increased first-round VC.

## Migration Data (IRS SOI 2021–2022)

- Top states gaining tax filers: FL (+125,551), TX (+88,216), NC (+43,653), SC (+32,927), TN (+30,935)
- Top states losing tax filers: CA (-144,203), NY (-108,586), IL (-45,460), MA (-26,033), NJ (-20,820)
- California lost $102 billion in AGI to outmigration from 2020–2022.

## State Conformity

As of March 2026: 33 states conform, 7 have no income tax, 6 are decoupled, 2 have pending decoupling legislation, 2 have partial conformity (Hawaii 50% exclusion, Massachusetts 3% rate).

## Source Documents

- [U.S. Treasury OTA Working Paper 127](https://home.treasury.gov/system/files/131/WP-127.pdf) (January 2025)
- [Edwards & Todenhaupt (2020)](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3520783)
- [Chen & Farre-Mensa (2023/2025)](https://ssrn.com/abstract=4482626)
- [IRS SOI Migration Data](https://www.irs.gov/statistics/soi-tax-stats-migration-data)
- [Tax Foundation Migration Analysis (Dec 2024)](https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/taxes-affect-state-migration-trends-2024/)
- [CA Legislative Analyst's Office (2024)](https://lao.ca.gov/Publications/Report/4932)
- [ITEP Section 1202 Analysis](https://itep.org/qsbs-trump-tax-law-threatens-state-revenues-enriches-wealthy/)

## Pages

- **Homepage** (https://qsbsfacts.org): Full single-page analysis with all sections, charts, and interactive calculator.
- **Evidence** (https://qsbsfacts.org/evidence): Academic evidence that QSBS drives startup formation and investment. Edwards & Todenhaupt (2020), Chen & Farre-Mensa (2023/2025).
- **Who Uses It** (https://qsbsfacts.org/who-uses-it): Treasury data on claimant demographics, income levels, exclusion amounts, and the 4 archetypes.
- **The 94% Claim** (https://qsbsfacts.org/the-94-percent-claim): Deconstruction of the ITEP "94% goes to millionaires" methodology and the dollar-weighted vs. people-counted distinction.
- **Calculator** (https://qsbsfacts.org/calculator): Interactive exit tax calculator for all 50 states + DC. Supports URL parameter sharing.
- **State Impact** (https://qsbsfacts.org/state-impact): State conformity map, IRS migration data, and California's $102B AGI loss from outmigration.
- **Sources** (https://qsbsfacts.org/sources): All citations, data sources, methodology notes, and links to primary documents.
